setting.note: Thirty years of Afghan history seen through two women married to the same brutal man in Kabul. Mariam grows up outside Herat and is married off to the shoemaker Rasheed; years later the educated Laila, orphaned when a rocket destroys her family's house, becomes his second wife, and the two women's alliance carries the book. The city is real and specific: Rasheed's house in the Deh-Mazang district, the dry-riverbed market called Titanic City, and the Ghazi Stadium where the Taliban hold their executions.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Khaled Hosseini, first published 2007. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A Thousand Splendid Suns, Khaled Hosseini (2007). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The gold of the thousand splendid suns of the poem in the title, the moons and suns hidden behind the walls of Kabul. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
